prs.title: Add Phase 4: Statistical Analysis notebook
prs.body:
- RFM customer segmentation using K-Means (k=4)
- Welch's t-test: delivery delay vs review score
- Pearson correlation: delivery time vs review score
- Churn analysis using recency threshold
- Category impact analysis (top 10 categories)
- Payment method impact analysis
- Final statistical summary with key findings

E2E: Improve Playwright test patterns in task-logs.spec.ts

labels.name: area:UI
labels.description: Related to UI/UX. For Frontend Developers.

prs.body:
Improve Playwright test patterns in [specs/task-logs.spec.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tests/e2e/specs/task-logs.spec.ts:0:0-0:0) and related page objects to align with Playwright Best Practices. 

This PR focuses on improving test patterns only and does not change test coverage or behavior.

**Changes included in this PR:**
- **State-based waiting**: Removed `waitForLoadState("networkidle")` in [verifyStateFiltering](cci:1://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tests/e2e/pages/TaskInstancesPage.ts:47:2-82:3) ([TaskInstancesPage.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tests/e2e/pages/TaskInstancesPage.ts:0:0-0:0)), relying instead on native `expect().toBeVisible()` checks for specific UI rendering.
- **Web-first assertions**: Converted manual assertions like `expect(await rows.count()).toBeGreaterThan(0)` to the recommended web-first assertion: `await expect(rows).not.toHaveCount(0)` in [TaskInstancesPage.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tests/e2e/pages/TaskInstancesPage.ts:0:0-0:0).
- **Flaky check fix**: Fixed the "flaky check" in [task-logs.spec.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tests/e2e/specs/task-logs.spec.ts:0:0-0:0) by explicitly ensuring that `logItems.first()` has rendered and is visible before running regex checks against the content of the `virtualizedList` component.
- **Enable Spec**: Removed [task-logs.spec.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tests/e2e/specs/task-logs.spec.ts:0:0-0:0) from the `testIgnore` array in [playwright.config.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/playwright.config.ts:0:0-0:0).

closes: #63964
related: #63036

prs.title: New hire/udaykumarchoudhary/task
prs.body:
## Description
This PR introduces the `log-analyzer-agent` task, a new data-processing challenge for Harbor.

### Task Overview
The Log Analyzer Agent task evaluates an AI agent's ability to read and process raw text logs, extract specific information based on keywords, and format the output as a structured JSON report. 

**Scenario:** The agent is provided with an application log file (`/app/docker.log`) containing timestamped log entries (INFO, ERROR, WARNING).
**Objective:** The agent must parse the file using only standard library tools to:
1. Count the total number of log lines.
2. Count the occurrences of `ERROR` and `WARNING` keywords.
3. Extract the exact error messages from the `ERROR` lines.
4. Output these findings into a newly created `/app/error_report.json` file in a strictly defined JSON schema.

### Difficulty & Categorization
- **Difficulty:** Easy
- **Category:** Data Processing
- **Tags:** [log](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/harbor/harbor_tasks/log-analyzer-agent/environment/docker.log:0:0-0:0), `files`, `parsing`, [json](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/harbor/harbor_tasks/log-analyzer-agent/environment/error_report.json:0:0-0:0)
- **Time Estimates:** 10 mins (Expert) / 30 mins (Junior)

### Implementation Details
- **Environment:** Basic Docker environment with a generated [docker.log](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/harbor/harbor_tasks/log-analyzer-agent/environment/docker.log:0:0-0:0) file placed in the working directory.
- **Verification:** Uses `pytest` with the `pytest-json-ctrf` plugin. The verifier script (`test_outputs.py`) checks that the [error_report.json](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/harbor/harbor_tasks/log-analyzer-agent/environment/error_report.json:0:0-0:0) file exists, is valid JSON, and exactly matches the expected counts and extracted string messages. [test.sh](cci:7://file:///Users/udaykumarchoudhary/Desktop/harbor/harbor_tasks/log-analyzer-agent/tests/test.sh:0:0-0:0) correctly executes the tests and captures the exit code to determine the task reward (1 for pass, 0 for fail).
- **Reference Solution:** A complete Bash-based reference solution (`solve.sh`) is provided using standard Unix text-processing tools (`grep`, `wc`, `jq`, etc.) to prove the task is solvable.

prs.title: fix(types): add [Symbol.iterator]() to NodeListOf (fixes #31382)
prs.body:
fix(types): add`[Symbol.iterator]()` to `NodeListOf`

This PR adds missing iterable support to `NodeListOf<TNode>` in the DOM
typings. Without this method, TypeScript raises error TS2488:

```ts
for (const child of div.childNodes) {}
// TS2488: NodeListOf<ChildNode> must have a [Symbol.iterator]() method
```

This brings Deno’s DOM types in line with upstream `lib.dom.d.ts`, where
`NodeListOf` is defined as iterable.


## Related Issue

Fixes #31382


## Details

The following method was added to `NodeListOf<TNode>`:

```ts
[Symbol.iterator](): IterableIterator<TNode>;
```

This is a type-only change and does not affect the runtime behavior of Deno.
The fix allows idiomatic iteration over `NodeListOf`, such as:

```ts
for (const child of element.childNodes) {
  console.log(child);
}
```

prs.title: feat: add engines field to package.json files
prs.body:
<!-- DO NOT IGNORE THE TEMPLATE!

Thank you for contributing!

Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:

- Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- Check that there isn't already a PR that solves the problem the same way to avoid creating a duplicate.
- Provide a description in this PR that addresses **what** the PR is solving, or reference the issue that it solves (e.g. `fixes #123`).
- Ideally, include relevant tests that fail without this PR but pass with it.

-->

### Description
  This pull request adds the engines field to all package.json
  files, setting the required Node.js version to >=20. This
  change addresses the issue of clarifying the supported
  Node.js versions for the project, ensuring consistency and
  preventing potential issues for contributors and users.

### Linked Issues
  Fixes #1024


### Additional context
None


<!-- e.g. is there anything you'd like reviewers to focus on? -->

prs.title: feat: replace image directives with text art (fixes #4708)
prs.body:
This PR fixes issue #4708 by replacing the image directives in the libuv.1 man page with text art. This ensures that the architecture
  and loop iteration diagrams are displayed correctly in the man page.

  The following changes were made:

   * Replaced the .. image:: directives in docs/src/design.rst with .. code-block:: directives containing the text art.
   * Removed the unused image files architecture.png and loop_iteration.png from the docs/src/static directory.

  This change adheres to the contribution guidelines and has been tested by building the documentation.

prs.title: feat: add KNN algorithm implementation
prs.body:
## Summary
- Implemented K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm from scratch.
- Added example usage with training and test datasets.
- Verified accuracy (100% on sample dataset).
- Created supporting files:
  - `knn.py` → core implementation of KNN
  - `requirements.txt` → dependencies (numpy, scikit-learn if needed)
  - `README.md` → instructions for running the code

## Changes
- New folder `Data-Mining-Algorithms` added.
- Included code for training, prediction, and evaluation.
- Easy to extend for other datasets.

## Testing
- Ran script locally with sample data.
- Output: `KNN Accuracy: 1.00`

## Next Steps
- Add visualization (decision boundary plot).
- Extend support for multi-class datasets.

* **Style**

prs.title: Fix: Rename to StackMate, Remove Emojis & Update UI Styling (#63, #64, #67)
prs.body:
#63  #64  #67 
This pull request addresses the following issues:

Removed all emojis from the extension to maintain a clean and professional UI.
Renamed the extension to "StackMate" and applied Figma-based styling:

Center-aligned heading

Consistent font weight and spacing

Updated "Stack Overflow" text color to solid black (text-black).

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added a dynamic and interactive file explorer ("TreeView") to the dashboard, allowing users to browse and copy file paths.

* **Enhancements**
  * Updated the app title and branding in all supported languages.
  * Improved the developer tip section to prevent repeated tips and added a refresh button with loading feedback.
  * Enhanced dashboard layout and replaced static tips with the new DevTip component.
  * Cleared search state when returning to the dashboard for improved user experience.

* **Style**
  * Unified color scheme in the StackOverflow component to black and white.
  * Removed emoji icons from various UI elements for a cleaner look.

* **Chores**
  * Removed unused helper functions and emoji from messages and translations.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Fix #51: Dev Tips Box UI
prs.body: - Added gradient text as per Figma design - Rounded box with shadow styling ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Introduced a visually rich, responsive, and interactive UI with new styles and theming. * Added a dashboard view with clickable tool cards and a "Dev tips" section. * Added pop-up overlays for color and language selection, and improved header controls. * Implemented a custom hook for note-taking with pinning and persistent storage. * Enhanced connection status indicator with animated dot and tooltip. * **Bug Fixes** * Corrected placement and structure of configuration fields for improved reliability. * **Refactor** * Modularized app into distinct views and centralized note logic for better maintainability. * **Chores** * Updated and added dependencies to support new features and CSS handling.

prs.title: Fix #50: Card Layout for Notes/Modules
prs.body: Implemented a responsive grid layout for notes/modules ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Introduced a visually rich and responsive UI with modern styling, including gradients, blur effects, and improved layouts. * Added a dashboard view with clickable tool cards for Notes, JSON Validator, and Stack Overflow. * Enhanced header with grouped controls for internet connection status, color picker, language selector, and theme toggle, each with interactive popups. * Implemented a compact, animated connection status indicator with tooltip. * **Refactor** * Centralized note management logic into a new custom hook for improved modularity and maintainability. * Migrated inline styles to CSS classes and adopted new iconography for a cohesive look. * **Chores** * Updated and added dependencies to support new styling and CSS imports. * Improved project configuration for CSS file handling.

prs.title: Fix #48: Apply Gradient Background & Global Styles
prs.body: - Added purple-to-dark gradient background - Set up global font and spacing as per Figma - Applied clean card shadows ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Introduced a comprehensive, responsive CSS stylesheet for enhanced visual design and theming. * Added a dashboard view with clickable tool cards and a developer tips section. * Implemented a custom hook for centralized note management with local storage support. * Redesigned header with logo, app title, and interactive controls for internet status, color picker, language selector, and theme toggle. * Improved connection status indicator with animated dot and tooltip. * **Refactor** * Modularized note management logic and reorganized the main app layout for a multi-view interface. * Migrated inline styles to CSS classes and updated UI structure for better maintainability. * **Chores** * Added new dependencies for icons and CSS handling, and updated development dependencies. * Updated build configuration to support importing CSS files.

prs.title: Update: Improve/Update navbar design and functionality
prs.body: #54 ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Introduced a comprehensive and responsive CSS stylesheet for enhanced app appearance, including dark mode and animated controls. * Added a modular dashboard view with clickable tool cards and a dev tips section. * Implemented pop-up overlays for color picker and language selector controls. * Added a compact, animated internet connection status indicator with tooltip. * **Improvements** * Refactored app state management and note-taking functionality into a custom hook for better modularity and local storage support. * Updated header design for improved navigation and grouped controls. * Enabled CSS file imports and bundling in the application build process. * **Chores** * Added and updated dependencies to support new features and CSS handling.

prs.title: Add JSON Formatter & Validator feature (#5)
prs.body:
#5 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ee2cf8eb-7bd8-46dc-adf0-881a9d2bba3f


- Paste or type raw JSON into a textarea.
- Click **Validate** to check for syntax errors.
  - Shows “✅ Valid JSON” or “❌ Invalid JSON: [error]”
- Click **Beautify** to format the JSON with proper indentation for better readability.

prs.title: Fix: Package.json syntax & update Webpack config for VS Code CSP compliance
prs.body:
### Changes Made
- Fixed syntax issue in `package.json` by adding the missing comma.
- Updated `webpack.config.js`:
  - `mode: production` for optimized builds
  - `publicPath: './'` for correct asset loading in VS Code webview
  - `devtool: "source-map"` for development debugging without CSP violations
  - Removed `eval()` usage to comply with VS Code CSP

### Why
- Fixed JSON parsing error.
- Prevented CSP-related errors in VS Code webview (`unsafe-eval` issue → blank screen fixed).
- Secure, stable setup for future development.
